articles.title: House race to save surveillance powers exposes Trump’s grip — and its limits
articles.summary: A late-night revolt by Republicans forced the House to settle for a short extension of a controversial spy authority, underscoring both Trump’s influence and the restiveness inside his party.

articles.title: IMF warns war shock could stall a fragile global recovery
articles.summary: The fund says the Middle East conflict is already lifting inflation risks and clouding a world economy that was still digesting tariff shocks.

articles.title: NASA’s Moonshot Is Now a Science Mission, Not Just a Dress Rehearsal
articles.summary: Artemis II’s lunar flyby is giving scientists a fresh look at ancient basins and helping preview how crewed missions could accelerate lunar geology.

articles.title: Cannes 2026 leans back toward the auteurs as Hollywood’s tentpoles stay home
articles.summary: With the 79th Cannes Film Festival set for May, this year’s lineup is signaling a return to prestige-first cinema even as studios flood the calendar with franchise plays.

articles.title: Anthropic’s latest Claude release underscores a new AI frontier: cybersecurity risk
articles.summary: Claude Opus 4.7 arrives as Anthropic simultaneously pushes a more capable coding model and warns that its newest frontier systems can be weaponized for cyberattacks.

articles.title: OpenAI’s GPT-5.4-Cyber launch shows how fast AI safety and security are colliding
articles.summary: The new model variant underscores a fast-moving market where AI companies are racing to arm defenders without widening the door for attackers.

articles.title: Labubu mania keeps spinning into 2026 as collectors, investors and skeptics collide
articles.summary: The viral plush monster has become more than a toy. It’s now a resale market, a fashion accessory and a test case for how far internet fandom can push a niche collectible into the mainstream.

articles.title: Sudan’s war enters a fourth year as a Berlin conference tests whether diplomacy can catch up
articles.summary: As fighting between the Sudanese army and the Rapid Support Forces grinds on, diplomats in Berlin are trying to convert a deepening humanitarian catastrophe into coordinated pressure for a ceasefire.

articles.title: Live Nation’s antitrust loss could reshape concert culture — but not overnight
articles.summary: A jury found Live Nation and Ticketmaster liable for monopolistic practices, a verdict that could eventually affect ticketing, fees and how fans buy seats to major shows.

articles.title: Lebanon’s ceasefire opens a narrow path back home, but fear and ruins remain
articles.summary: A 10-day truce has eased bombardment in Lebanon and prompted thousands of displaced families to start returning, yet uncertainty over the deal, Israeli warnings and the scale of destruction are clouding hopes for a durable calm.
